forecast-period.text: A weak high pressure system south of WA will move eastwards over waters south of SA on Sunday. This high will drift further eastwards to the Tasman Sea on Monday as a low pressure system develops in the vicinity of the Bight. The low and associated fronts are expected to move slowly southeastwards along the SA coast next week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Southerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots inshore in the late morning. Winds turning east to southeasterly below 10 knots in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly about 10 knots increasing to 10 to 15 knots before dawn. Winds reaching up to 20 knots offshore during the day.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 2 metres by early evening.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly during the morning.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: Southerly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Easterly 10 to 15 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 2 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots tending northerly during the morning.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Streaky Bay during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.text: South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots tending south to southeasterly below 10 knots in the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 metres.
Mostly sunny.
East to southeasterly below 10 knots tending east to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots before dawn.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the late afternoon.
Around 1 metre, decreasing below 0.5 metres later in the evening.
Southwesterly 3 metres.
Mostly sunny.
Variable below 10 knots becoming northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending northerly 15 to 20 knots during the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres in the west during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Southeast to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the evening.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon, then increasing to 3 to 4 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy.
Variable about 10 knots becoming northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2.5 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly around 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: West to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots tending south to southwesterly below 10 knots in the late morning and afternoon. Winds reaching up to 20 knots offshore in the early afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre around midday.
Southwesterly 3 metres.
Cloudy. 70% chance of rain.
Variable about 10 knots becoming northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly during the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: Westerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Port MacDonnell in the afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
West to southwesterly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres south of Beachport.
Partly cloudy.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the early morning then becoming northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Cloudy. near 100% chance of rain north of Cowell to Wallaroo, 60% chance elsewhere.
Southeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending easterly in the early morning then tending northeasterly in the late evening.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Mostly sunny.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly 15 to 20 knots during the day.
Around 1 metre.
South to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
West to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots shifting southerly in the early afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Cloudy. 95% chance of rain.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable below 10 knots before dawn then becoming east to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Mostly sunny.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly in the late afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of rain.
Variable below 10 knots becoming east to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly during the day.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
